facts.text: The Ramones played their first gig at CBGB in New York City on August 16, 1974.
The Sex Pistols released their only studio album, 'Never Mind the Bollocks, Here's the Sex Pistols,' in 1977.
The Clash were often referred to as 'The Only Band That Matters' by their fans and the press.
CBGB stands for Country, Bluegrass, and Blues, despite becoming the birthplace of American punk rock.
The Ramones adopted a shared surname to create a sense of unity, though none of them were actually related.
The term 'punk rock' was first used in print by rock critic Dave Marsh in Creem magazine in 1971.
The Sex Pistols' 'God Save the Queen' was banned by the BBC but still reached number two on the UK charts in 1977.
The Clash's 'London Calling' album was ranked by Rolling Stone as one of the greatest albums of all time.
Punk rock fashion often includes leather jackets, ripped jeans, band T-shirts, and Doc Martens boots.
The Buzzcocks released the first independently produced punk record, the 'Spiral Scratch' EP, in January 1977.
Black Flag was founded by guitarist Greg Ginn in Hermosa Beach, California, in 1976.
The DIY ethic in punk encourages bands to self-produce, self-release, and self-distribute their music.
Minor Threat's song 'Straight Edge' inadvertently launched an entire subculture of drug-free and alcohol-free punks.
The Dead Kennedys' frontman Jello Biafra ran for mayor of San Francisco in 1979 and finished fourth.
Punk zines like 'Sniffin' Glue' and 'Slash' were crucial in spreading punk culture before the internet era.
The mohawk hairstyle became a punk icon in the late 1970s as a symbol of rebellion and nonconformity.
SST Records, founded by Black Flag's Greg Ginn in 1978, became one of the most important independent punk labels.
The Ramones' debut album was recorded in just over two weeks for approximately $6,400.
Dischord Records was co-founded by Ian MacKaye of Minor Threat in Washington, D.C., in 1980.
The Sex Pistols' bassist Sid Vicious could barely play his instrument when he joined the band.
The Clash's 'London Calling' album cover was inspired by Elvis Presley's debut album artwork.
Punk rock emerged in the mid-1970s as a reaction against the perceived excesses of mainstream rock music.
The New York Dolls are considered proto-punk pioneers who influenced the Ramones and the Sex Pistols.
Safety pins became a punk fashion accessory after being popularized by the Sex Pistols and their fans.
The Damned released the first UK punk single, 'New Rose,' on October 22, 1976.
Iggy Pop and the Stooges are widely regarded as one of the most important proto-punk bands.
The Ramones played over 2,200 concerts during their career, often at a relentless touring pace.
Epitaph Records was founded by Bad Religion guitarist Brett Gurewitz in 1981 in a Los Angeles garage.
The straight edge movement, originating from Minor Threat, advocates abstinence from alcohol, tobacco, and drugs.
Punk rock concerts are known for moshing, stage diving, and crowd surfing.
The Misfits blended punk rock with horror film imagery, creating the horror punk subgenre.
Johnny Rotten auditioned for the Sex Pistols by miming along to Alice Cooper's 'I'm Eighteen' in Malcolm McLaren's shop.
X-Ray Spex, fronted by Poly Styrene, were one of the first punk bands to feature a woman of color as lead vocalist.
Crass combined punk music with anarchist politics, helping to define anarcho-punk in the late 1970s.
The Descendents are credited with being one of the earliest pop-punk bands, influencing groups like Blink-182.
Bad Brains were a pioneering all-Black hardcore punk band from Washington, D.C., who also incorporated reggae.
The Germs' frontman Darby Crash died of a heroin overdose on December 7, 1980.
Maximum Rocknroll was an influential punk zine and radio show that ran from 1982 until 2019.
The punk subculture often embraces anti-establishment views and challenges societal norms.
Television's 'Marquee Moon' is considered a landmark album that bridged punk and art rock.
Patti Smith's 'Horses,' released in 1975, is often cited as one of the first punk rock albums.
The Undertones released 'Teenage Kicks' in 1978, which became BBC DJ John Peel's all-time favorite song.
Richard Hell is credited with pioneering the punk look of spiky hair, torn clothes, and safety pins.
Stiff Little Fingers were one of the first punk bands from Northern Ireland, often addressing the Troubles in their lyrics.
Green Day's 'Dookie' album, released in 1994, brought pop-punk into the mainstream and sold over 10 million copies.
The Offspring's 'Smash' became the best-selling independent label album of all time when released in 1994.
Operation Ivy, from the East Bay area of California, were pioneers of ska punk and influenced Rancid.
Fat Wreck Chords was founded by NOFX frontman Fat Mike in 1990.
Bikini Kill, fronted by Kathleen Hanna, were central to the riot grrrl movement of the early 1990s.
The riot grrrl movement combined punk music with feminist activism and DIY media.
Sleater-Kinney emerged from the riot grrrl scene in Olympia, Washington, and became one of the most acclaimed rock bands of the 1990s.
Fugazi, led by Ian MacKaye, famously kept their concert ticket prices at five dollars throughout their career.
The Minutemen from San Pedro, California, blended punk with funk, jazz, and spoken word.
Hüsker Dü helped bridge the gap between hardcore punk and alternative rock in the 1980s.
The Replacements started as a chaotic punk band in Minneapolis before evolving into influential alternative rockers.
NOFX's 'Punk in Drublic' is one of the best-selling independent punk albums, with over one million copies sold.
Rancid's '...And Out Come the Wolves' is considered one of the definitive punk albums of the 1990s.
Blink-182's 'Enema of the State' sold over 15 million copies worldwide and helped define pop-punk.
Agnostic Front are considered godfathers of New York hardcore punk, forming in 1980.
Henry Rollins joined Black Flag as vocalist in 1981 after being a fan who jumped on stage during a show.
The Circle Jerks were formed by ex-Black Flag vocalist Keith Morris in 1979.
Bad Religion have been releasing music since 1980 and are known for their intellectual lyrics and vocal harmonies.
Suicidal Tendencies blended punk, thrash metal, and funk, becoming one of the first crossover bands.
The Adolescents' self-titled debut is often called the 'Blue Album' and is a classic of Southern California punk.
Discharge's 'Hear Nothing See Nothing Say Nothing' was hugely influential on thrash metal and grindcore.
Cock Sparrer formed in 1972 in London and are considered forefathers of Oi! punk and street punk.
Sham 69's Jimmy Pursey was one of the leading voices of the working-class punk Oi! movement.
Siouxsie and the Banshees started in the punk scene before becoming pioneering post-punk and goth artists.
Joy Division emerged from the punk scene in Manchester, England, and became one of the most influential post-punk bands.
Wire's debut album 'Pink Flag' contained 21 tracks, many under a minute long, exemplifying punk's brevity.
Gang of Four merged punk energy with funk rhythms and Marxist politics on their debut 'Entertainment!'
Bauhaus are often credited with launching goth rock with their 1979 single 'Bela Lugosi's Dead.'
The Fall, led by Mark E. Smith, released over 30 studio albums and are cult icons of post-punk.
Killing Joke's aggressive sound influenced industrial rock, metal, and bands like Nirvana and Nine Inch Nails.
Public Image Ltd (PiL) was John Lydon's post-Sex Pistols band, exploring experimental post-punk sounds.
The Cure started as a punk band before evolving into one of the defining acts of goth and post-punk.
Punk rock songs are typically short, fast, and feature simple chord progressions, often using power chords.
The average Ramones song was about two minutes long, reflecting punk's emphasis on brevity and energy.
Malcolm McLaren, manager of the Sex Pistols, was a key figure in shaping punk's visual identity and controversy.
Vivienne Westwood designed many of the iconic punk fashion pieces sold at her and McLaren's shop SEX.
The Sex Pistols performed their first gig on November 6, 1975, at Saint Martin's School of Art.
The 100 Club Punk Special, held in London on September 20-21, 1976, was one of the first punk festivals.
The Damned were the first UK punk band to release a single, an album, and to tour the United States.
The Stranglers were older than most punk contemporaries and blended punk with keyboards and a darker sound.
Generation X, fronted by Billy Idol, combined punk with pop hooks before Idol went on to solo fame.
The Jam, led by Paul Weller, blended punk energy with mod influences and became one of the biggest UK bands of the late 1970s.
The Slits were an all-female punk band who incorporated reggae and dub into their sound.
The Raincoats blended punk with experimental and folk influences, later championed by Kurt Cobain.
Poly Styrene of X-Ray Spex was one of the first punk frontpeople to openly address consumerism in lyrics.
Fear's performance on Saturday Night Live on October 31, 1981, caused significant damage to the studio.
Black Flag's iconic four-bar logo was designed by Raymond Pettibon, Greg Ginn's brother.
Raymond Pettibon's artwork for SST Records defined the visual aesthetic of American hardcore punk.
MDC (Millions of Dead Cops) were a politically charged hardcore punk band from Austin, Texas.
Reagan Youth combined hardcore punk with satirical commentary on the Reagan era's politics.
Negative Approach from Detroit were one of the most intense hardcore bands of the early 1980s.
Government Issue were a prominent Washington, D.C. hardcore band closely associated with Dischord Records.
Rites of Spring are credited as one of the first emo bands, emerging from the D.C. hardcore scene in 1984.
The term 'emo' originated as short for 'emotional hardcore,' describing bands like Rites of Spring.
Scream were a D.C. punk band whose drummer Dave Grohl later joined Nirvana and founded Foo Fighters.
Youth of Today were central to the New York straight edge hardcore scene and the Youth Crew movement.
Sick of It All have been a mainstay of the New York hardcore scene since forming in 1986.
Madball formed in the late 1980s with vocalist Freddy Cricien, who was only ten years old at the time.
The Refused's 'The Shape of Punk to Come' was initially a commercial failure but later hailed as a classic.
At the Drive-In from El Paso, Texas, were a pivotal post-hardcore band before splitting into the Mars Volta and Sparta.
Jawbreaker were a seminal band in the 1990s punk scene, blending hardcore with emotive songwriting.
Hot Water Music formed in Gainesville, Florida, in 1993 and became leaders of the gruff punk sound.
Against Me!'s Laura Jane Grace came out as a transgender woman in 2012, becoming a prominent LGBTQ+ figure in punk.
Lifetime from New Jersey are considered one of the founding bands of the melodic hardcore genre.
Propagandhi are known for combining technical punk rock with leftist political lyrics.
Millencolin are a Swedish punk band who have been active since 1992 and are prominent in European punk.
Me First and the Gimme Gimmes are a punk cover band supergroup featuring members of NOFX and Lagwagon.
The Warped Tour, founded by Kevin Lyman in 1995, became the biggest traveling punk rock festival in the world.
The last full cross-country Warped Tour took place in 2018 after 23 years of annual tours.
Lookout! Records, based in Berkeley, California, was the original label for Green Day and Operation Ivy.
Revelation Records, founded in 1987, became a cornerstone label for hardcore and straight edge punk.
Hellcat Records is a subsidiary of Epitaph founded by Tim Armstrong of Rancid in 1997.
Alternative Tentacles Records was founded by Jello Biafra of the Dead Kennedys in 1979.
The Decline of Western Civilization, directed by Penelope Spheeris in 1981, captured the early LA punk scene.
D.O.A. from Vancouver are often called the founders of hardcore punk, coining the term with their 1981 album.
Refused are from Umeå, Sweden, a small city that produced many influential hardcore bands.
Turbonegro from Norway blended punk with hard rock and glam to create their self-described 'deathpunk' style.
Ratos de Porão are one of the most important punk bands from Brazil, active since 1981.
Los Crudos were a Latino hardcore punk band from Chicago who sang exclusively in Spanish.
Amebix blended anarcho-punk with heavy metal to create what is now called crust punk.
Crust punk combines the heaviness of metal with the raw aggression and political ethos of anarcho-punk.
The Casualties are a New York street punk band known for their extreme mohawks and studded denim.
Leftöver Crack blended punk with ska, crack rock, and squatter culture in Lower East Side New York.
Dropkick Murphys combined punk rock with Irish folk music, pioneering Celtic punk in Boston.
Flogging Molly, fronted by Dublin-born Dave King, blend Celtic folk music with punk rock.
The Pogues, led by Shane MacGowan, are often credited as the originators of Celtic punk in the 1980s.
Punk rock in Northern Ireland was seen as a unifying force that crossed sectarian divides during the Troubles.
The Saints released '(I'm) Stranded' in September 1976, making it one of the earliest punk singles.
Radio Birdman from Sydney combined punk with Detroit rock influences like the Stooges and MC5.
CBGB closed its doors permanently on October 15, 2006, after 33 years of operation.
The final show at CBGB was performed by Patti Smith on October 15, 2006.
Gilman Street, officially 924 Gilman, is a volunteer-run punk venue in Berkeley that opened in 1986.
Green Day played some of their earliest shows at 924 Gilman Street before achieving mainstream success.
Punk house shows became a staple of the DIY touring circuit, with bands playing in basements and living rooms.
Book Your Own Fuckin' Life was a DIY punk resource guide listing venues, promoters, and labels worldwide.
Fugazi documented all of their shows and released an extensive archive of live recordings.
Ian MacKaye has kept Dischord Records operating from his home in Arlington, Virginia, for over four decades.
The straight edge X symbol originated from underage club-goers marked with an X on their hands to prevent alcohol purchases.
Earth Crisis from Syracuse, New York, combined straight edge ideology with vegan activism and metallic hardcore.
The first Punk Rock Bowling festival was held in Las Vegas in 1999 and has become an annual tradition.
Fest is an annual punk rock festival held in Gainesville, Florida, featuring hundreds of bands.
Rebellion Festival, held annually in Blackpool, England, is one of the largest punk festivals in the world.
The Ramones were inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame on March 18, 2002.
Joey Ramone passed away on April 15, 2001, from lymphoma.
Dee Dee Ramone died on June 5, 2002, from a heroin overdose.
Johnny Ramone passed away on September 15, 2004, from prostate cancer.
Tommy Ramone, the last surviving original Ramone, died on July 11, 2014.
Joe Strummer of the Clash died unexpectedly on December 22, 2002, from an undiagnosed heart defect.
The Clash broke up in 1986, but their influence on punk, reggae-punk fusion, and political rock endures.
The Clash's 'Sandinista!' was a triple album that explored reggae, jazz, gospel, and hip-hop.
The Sex Pistols lasted only about two and a half years as a band, from 1975 to early 1978.
The Sex Pistols reunited for the Filthy Lucre Tour in 1996, openly admitting it was for the money.
Sid Vicious of the Sex Pistols died of a heroin overdose on February 2, 1979, at age 21.
Never Mind the Bollocks debuted at number one on the UK album charts despite widespread bans and censorship.
The word 'bollocks' in the Sex Pistols' album title led to obscenity charges against a record shop owner, who was acquitted.
The Buzzcocks' Pete Shelley wrote some of punk's most heartfelt love songs, including 'Ever Fallen in Love.'
Pete Shelley of the Buzzcocks passed away on December 6, 2018.
Sniffin' Glue was started by Mark Perry in July 1976 and ran for only 12 issues.
Punk Planet was a Chicago-based punk zine and magazine that ran from 1994 to 2007.
The punk ethic of 'anyone can do it' encouraged people with no training to start bands, zines, and labels.
Jamie Reid designed the Sex Pistols' iconic ransom-note style 'God Save the Queen' artwork.
The Misfits' Crimson Ghost skull logo is one of the most recognizable images in punk rock.
Glenn Danzig left the Misfits in 1983 to form Samhain and later the heavy metal band Danzig.
The original Misfits lineup of Danzig, Only, and Doyle reunited for a series of shows starting in 2016.
Punk rock was a major influence on the development of grunge, including bands like Nirvana and Mudhoney.
Kurt Cobain of Nirvana was deeply influenced by punk bands like the Raincoats, the Vaselines, and Black Flag.
The Pixies blended punk with surf rock and pop melodies, influencing alternative rock and Nirvana.
Sonic Youth started in the New York no wave and punk scenes before becoming icons of alternative rock.
Punk rock has a strong connection to skateboarding culture, particularly through bands like JFA and the Faction.
Skate punk features fast tempos, technical guitar work, and lyrics often referencing skate culture.
Burning Heart Records from Sweden released key albums by the Hives, Refused, and Millencolin.
Anti-Flag from Pittsburgh combine punk rock with anti-war and human rights activism.
Rise Against blended melodic hardcore with political activism and became one of punk's biggest mainstream acts.
The Distillers, fronted by Brody Dalle, reignited interest in female-led punk bands in the early 2000s.
The Interrupters brought ska-punk back to prominence in the 2010s with their Epitaph-released albums.
Sum 41 from Canada blended pop-punk with thrash metal influences.
New Found Glory are considered one of the defining pop-punk bands of the early 2000s.
Saves the Day's 'Through Being Cool' is a landmark album in emo-influenced pop-punk.
The Get Up Kids blended emo and pop-punk to create a sound that influenced many later bands.
Jimmy Eat World's 'Bleed American' brought emo-tinged punk rock to mainstream radio in 2001.
Thursday's 'Full Collapse' was a pivotal post-hardcore album that influenced the mid-2000s emo scene.
My Chemical Romance drew from punk, goth, and theatrical rock, achieving massive commercial success.
Fall Out Boy moved from the Chicago hardcore scene to become one of the biggest pop-punk acts of the 2000s.
Pete Wentz of Fall Out Boy was in the hardcore band Arma Angelus before forming Fall Out Boy.
Drive-Thru Records was a key label in the early 2000s pop-punk boom.
Fueled by Ramen, originally a small punk label, grew to sign Paramore, Fall Out Boy, and Panic! at the Disco.
The Suicide Machines combined punk, ska, and hardcore in the 1990s Detroit scene.
Less Than Jake from Gainesville, Florida, were a long-running ska-punk band active since 1992.
The Mighty Mighty Bosstones helped bring ska-punk to mainstream audiences with 'The Impression That I Get.'
Reel Big Fish became one of the most popular third-wave ska bands with 'Turn the Radio Off' in 1996.
Travis Barker of Blink-182 originally played drums in the Aquabats before joining Blink.
Pop-punk experienced a revival in the early 2020s with bands like Turnstile and older acts reuniting.
Turnstile from Baltimore blended hardcore punk with pop, indie, and R&B influences on 'Glow On.'
The Dead Kennedys' 'Holiday in Cambodia' is one of the most politically pointed songs in punk history.
The Dead Kennedys faced an obscenity trial in 1986 over artwork included with their album 'Frankenchrist.'
Bad Religion's Greg Graffin holds a Ph.D. in zoology from Cornell University and has taught at UCLA.
Bad Religion's 'Suffer' album, released in 1988, is credited with reviving the Southern California punk scene.
Pennywise's 'Bro Hymn' has become an anthem played at punk shows and sporting events worldwide.
NOFX's Fat Mike produced the Rock Against Bush compilation series in 2004.
Punk rock has a long tradition of benefit concerts for social causes.
Rock Against Racism was a UK movement in the late 1970s that used punk and reggae to combat rising racism.
Crass ran their own Dial House commune in Essex, England, living according to anarchist principles.
Chumbawamba started as an anarcho-punk band before achieving mainstream success with 'Tubthumping' in 1997.
Punk ethics influenced the formation of cooperatively run spaces, record labels, and community organizations.
Food Not Bombs, though not exclusively punk, has deep roots in the punk DIY community.
Short Music for Short People was a 1999 Fat Wreck Chords release with 101 songs, each 30 seconds or less.
The Ramones' 'Blitzkrieg Bop' uses the chant 'Hey! Ho! Let's Go!' which is one of punk's most iconic phrases.
The Clash's 'Should I Stay or Should I Go' became their only UK number one hit when rereleased in 1991.
Green Day's 'American Idiot' was a rock opera concept album later adapted into a Broadway musical.
American Idiot the musical opened on Broadway on April 20, 2010.
Green Day were inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame on April 18, 2015.
The Offspring's 'Self Esteem' became one of the most-played rock songs on radio in 1994.
Rancid turned down a major label deal to stay on Epitaph Records, earning respect from the punk community.
Matt Freeman of Rancid is widely regarded as one of the most skilled bass players in punk history.
Bad Religion have released seventeen studio albums, making them one of the most prolific punk bands.
Joan Jett started in the all-female punk-influenced band the Runaways before her successful solo career.
Patti Smith's 'Because the Night,' co-written with Bruce Springsteen, became her biggest hit.
Blondie emerged from the CBGB punk scene and achieved worldwide pop success.
Talking Heads were a CBGB band who blended punk with art rock, funk, and world music.
Richard Hell's 'Blank Generation' became an anthem of the New York punk scene in 1977.
The Dead Boys' 'Sonic Reducer' is one of the classic songs of the early American punk movement.
Johnny Thunders of the New York Dolls and the Heartbreakers was a tragic figure in punk history.
Wayne County (later Jayne County) was a pioneer of punk and transgender visibility in 1970s New York.
The Plasmatics, fronted by Wendy O. Williams, were known for extreme stage shows involving chainsaws.
The Avengers were a short-lived but important San Francisco punk band fronted by Penelope Houston.
Flipper from San Francisco played deliberately slow, noisy punk that anticipated sludge and noise rock.
Dead Kennedys formed in San Francisco in June 1978.
Middle Class from Santa Ana may have recorded the first American hardcore punk single in 1978.
Minutemen's D. Boon died in a van accident on December 22, 1985.
Minutemen's 'Double Nickels on the Dime' is considered a masterpiece of American independent music.
Mike Watt of the Minutemen continues to be one of punk's most active and respected musicians.
The Meat Puppets blended punk with country, psychedelia, and folk on early SST Records releases.
Descendents' 'Milo Goes to College' is named after vocalist Milo Aukerman's academic pursuits.
Milo Aukerman of the Descendents earned a Ph.D. in biochemistry while continuing to front the band.
7 Seconds from Reno, Nevada, were one of the most important positive hardcore bands of the 1980s.
Steve Albini, who started in the punk band Big Black, became one of the most important music producers.
Punk's influence extends to visual art, graphic design, fashion, filmmaking, and political activism.
Vivienne Westwood's punk-inspired fashion designs became a major force in the global fashion industry.
Studded belts, bullet belts, and band patches on denim and leather are staples of punk fashion.
Doc Martens boots became synonymous with punk and skinhead culture in the UK.
The leather jacket has been a punk staple since the Ramones adopted it as their uniform.
Punk venues often have strict policies against violence, racism, sexism, and homophobia.
Converge's 'Jane Doe' is widely regarded as one of the most important hardcore albums of the 2000s.
The Dillinger Escape Plan combined hardcore punk with complex time signatures and dissonance.
Touché Amoré from Los Angeles represent a modern wave of emotional post-hardcore punk.
Title Fight from Kingston, Pennsylvania, evolved from punk and hardcore into shoegaze-influenced music.
The Wonder Years from Philadelphia craft anthemic punk rock with deeply personal lyrics.
The Menzingers from Scranton, Pennsylvania, are noted for their heartland punk sound.
PUP from Toronto combine high-energy punk with catchy melodies and sardonic humor.
Jeff Rosenstock releases music on a pay-what-you-want model, continuing the punk DIY tradition.
AJJ (formerly Andrew Jackson Jihad) blend folk punk with dark humor and existential themes.
Folk punk combines acoustic instruments and folk song structures with punk energy and attitude.
Gogol Bordello blend punk with Romani music, led by Ukrainian-born frontman Eugene Hütz.
The Beastie Boys were originally a hardcore punk band before pivoting to hip-hop.
Afro-punk is a movement celebrating Black punk musicians, emphasizing diversity and inclusivity.
Death from Detroit were an all-Black proto-punk band whose recordings were rediscovered decades later.
IDLES from Bristol, England, became one of the most celebrated modern punk bands.
Fontaines D.C. from Dublin brought post-punk back into mainstream attention in the late 2010s.
Amyl and the Sniffers from Melbourne deliver raw, energetic garage punk.
Queercore emerged in the 1980s as a movement of LGBTQ+ punk musicians challenging homophobia.
G.L.O.S.S. were a trans-fronted hardcore band from Olympia, Washington.
The Dead Kennedys' 'Nazi Punks Fuck Off' remains one of the most direct anti-racist statements in punk.
The Sex Pistols' Bill Grundy interview on December 1, 1976, caused a national scandal in Britain.
The Anarchy in the UK tour in late 1976 featured the Sex Pistols, the Clash, the Damned, and the Heartbreakers.
Punk rock helped democratize music by proving that technical virtuosity was not required for artistic expression.
The Exploited formed in Edinburgh in 1979 and became leaders of the UK82 street punk movement.
GBH from Birmingham helped create street punk in the late 1970s UK scene.
Social Distortion's Mike Ness has been with the band since 1978, one of punk's longest-serving frontmen.
D.R.I. were pioneers of crossover thrash, merging hardcore punk and thrash metal.
The Subhumans from the UK combined anarcho-punk with politically charged lyrics.
Magazine, fronted by ex-Buzzcocks member Howard Devoto, were pioneers of post-punk.
The Birthday Party, featuring Nick Cave, were a confrontational Australian post-punk band.
The Masque was a crucial early punk venue in Los Angeles, located in a Hollywood basement.
Dag Nasty featured guitarist Brian Baker, who had previously been in Minor Threat.
SSD were a key band in the Boston hardcore scene of the early 1980s.
Naked Raygun were influential Chicago punk rockers who inspired many later alternative bands.
The Cro-Mags blended hardcore punk with metal and Hare Krishna spirituality in the 1980s.
Gorilla Biscuits were a key band in the late 1980s New York straight edge hardcore scene.
The Bouncing Souls formed in New Brunswick, New Jersey, in 1987 and are still active.
Pennywise formed in Hermosa Beach, California, in 1988.
AFI started as a hardcore punk band before transitioning to a gothic and alternative sound.
Stiff Little Fingers' 'Inflammable Material' was the first album on the Rough Trade label in 1979.
The Ruts from London blended punk with reggae and are known for 'Babylon's Burning.'
Punk in Australia included bands like the Saints and Radio Birdman from the mid-1970s.
The Whisky a Go Go on the Sunset Strip hosted early punk shows.
Dischord Records has a policy of keeping all of its releases in print and available.
Have Heart were a straight edge hardcore band from Boston active from 2002 to 2009.
Blondie's Debbie Harry incorporated rap into a punk-adjacent pop song with 'Rapture.'
The Dictators are considered proto-punk pioneers from New York who predated the Ramones.
The Sex Pistols' 'Anarchy in the U.K.' was released on November 26, 1976.
The Clash released their self-titled debut album on April 8, 1977.
The Ramones' 'Rocket to Russia' was released on November 4, 1977.
Black Flag released the landmark hardcore album 'Damaged' on December 5, 1981.
Minor Threat's self-titled EP was released in June 1981 on Dischord Records.
Rancid released '...And Out Come the Wolves' on August 22, 1995.
The Offspring released 'Smash' on April 8, 1994, selling over 11 million copies worldwide.
The Clash's 'London Calling' was released on December 14, 1979, in the UK.
Winston Smith created surreal collage artwork for the Dead Kennedys and other punk bands.
Punk album cover art often uses collage, ransom-note lettering, and stark imagery.
Touch and Go Records from Chicago released records by Big Black, the Jesus Lizard, and Shellac.
Asian Man Records was founded by Mike Park and became a home for DIY punk and ska.
SNFU from Edmonton, Alberta, were a long-running Canadian punk band.
The Hives from Sweden brought high-energy garage punk to international audiences.
Rudimentary Peni combined anarcho-punk with goth influences and frontman Nick Blinko's artwork.
Doom from Birmingham, England, are considered one of the founding bands of crust punk.
Choking Victim released one influential album, 'No Gods/No Managers,' before becoming Leftöver Crack.
Conflict were a prominent anarcho-punk band closely associated with Crass.
The punk rock community has always valued accessibility and low ticket prices.
Razorcake is a DIY punk fanzine based in Los Angeles that has been running since 2001.
Profane Existence is an anarcho-punk collective and label founded in Minneapolis in 1989.
Slash Records started as a punk zine before becoming a label that signed the Germs and X.
Jade Tree Records released important records by the Promise Ring and Jets to Brazil.
Bridge Nine Records continued the tradition of East Coast hardcore.
Crass Records released records at cost price, rejecting the profit motive of the mainstream music industry.
Punk houses and squats have served as community spaces, venues, and homes for touring musicians.
Anti-Racist Action groups have been closely tied to punk and hardcore scenes since the 1980s.
The Great Rock 'n' Roll Swindle was a 1980 film about the Sex Pistols.
The Buzzcocks pioneered the self-released single, inspiring countless DIY punk releases.
Dinosaur Jr. combined punk energy with loud guitar feedback and classic rock influences.
Good Riddance were a political skate punk band from Santa Cruz, California.
Strike Anywhere from Richmond, Virginia, combined melodic punk with politically engaged lyrics.
Ceremony from Rohnert Park, California, evolved from hardcore to post-punk over their career.
Shame from London are part of the new wave of British post-punk.
Trash Talk from Sacramento brought back raw hardcore punk aggression in the late 2000s.
Streetlight Manifesto pushed ska-punk in a progressive direction.
The punk scene has historically wrestled with issues of inclusivity regarding race, gender, and sexuality.
Pansy Division are a pop-punk band who have been openly gay since forming in 1991.
Sharp skins (Skinheads Against Racial Prejudice) fought neo-Nazi infiltration of punk and skinhead scenes.
The Offspring's Dexter Holland has a Ph.D. in molecular biology and is also a licensed pilot.
Punk shows often feature between-song banter where vocalists share political views or personal stories.
The Replacements were notorious for their shambolic live performances, sometimes playing entire sets drunk.
Fugazi never sold merchandise, considering it contrary to their anti-commercial punk principles.
The Nomeansno from Victoria, British Columbia, blended punk with progressive rock and jazz.
Subhumans' 'The Day the Country Died' is considered one of the essential anarcho-punk albums.
The Damned's Dave Vanian adopted a vampire-inspired look that influenced the goth subculture.
Aus-Rotten were a politically active crust punk band from Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.
The Weirdos were a key band in the early Los Angeles punk scene of the late 1970s.
Penelope Spheeris' documentaries are considered the definitive film records of the LA punk and metal scenes.
Bad Brains' debut album is widely considered one of the greatest hardcore punk records ever made.
The Vibrators were early participants in the London punk scene and played with the Sex Pistols in 1976.
Henry Rollins has become a noted author, spoken word artist, and actor since his days in Black Flag.
Circle Jerks' 'Wild in the Streets' is a classic 45-second blast of hardcore punk energy.
T.S.O.L. (True Sounds of Liberty) were part of the early 1980s Orange County punk scene.
Ian MacKaye has never used alcohol or drugs in his life, embodying the straight edge philosophy he helped create.
The Varukers formed in 1979 and are one of the longest-running UK hardcore punk bands.
Angelic Upstarts combined Oi! punk with socialist politics in the late 1970s northeast England scene.
The Rezillos were a colorful Scottish punk and new wave band known for their campy sci-fi aesthetic.
The Skids were a Scottish punk band whose guitarist Stuart Adamson later formed Big Country.
Echo & the Bunnymen came from the Liverpool post-punk scene alongside the Teardrop Explodes.
The Au Pairs combined post-punk with feminist and political themes in early 1980s Birmingham.
Flux of Pink Indians were an anarcho-punk band on Crass Records known for their experimental approach.
Articles of Faith were one of the most respected bands in the 1980s Chicago hardcore scene.
Die Creuzen from Milwaukee blended hardcore punk with progressive and experimental elements.
Jerry's Kids were part of the Boston hardcore punk scene alongside SSD and Gang Green.
Void from Maryland combined hardcore punk with heavy metal on their landmark Dischord split LP.
Embrace, featuring Ian MacKaye, were an early emo-associated band from the D.C. punk scene.
Snapcase from Buffalo, New York, were part of the 1990s post-hardcore movement.
Kid Dynamite were a short-lived but influential melodic hardcore band from Philadelphia.
Paint It Black from Philadelphia feature ex-Kid Dynamite vocalist Dan Yemin.
Avail from Richmond, Virginia, were beloved in the DIY punk community for their energetic live shows.
No Use for a Name were a melodic punk band on Fat Wreck Chords.
Lagwagon's Joey Cape is respected for both his band and solo acoustic work.
Strung Out combined punk with progressive and technical guitar work.
The Flatliners from Toronto evolved from ska-punk to melodic punk rock.
Iceage from Copenhagen brought a dark post-punk revival to the 2010s.
Mischief Brew from Philadelphia blended folk punk with anarchist politics.
The World/Inferno Friendship Society combined punk with cabaret and klezmer.
Pure Hell from Philadelphia were one of the first all-Black punk bands, forming in 1974.
Limp Wrist are a prominent queercore hardcore band fronted by Martin Sorrondeguy of Los Crudos.
Team Dresch were a queercore band from Portland active in the 1990s riot grrrl-adjacent scene.
Days N Daze from Houston are prominent in the modern folk punk and crust punk scenes.
Bomb the Music Industry! gave away all their music for free online, embodying DIY punk ethos.
Green Day's 'Dookie' was released on February 1, 1994, on Reprise Records.
Blink-182's self-titled album was released on November 18, 2003.
The Warped Tour's first edition took place on July 4, 1995.
Bad Religion's 'Suffer' was released on September 6, 1988, reigniting the SoCal punk scene.
